hypothesis: E625's stated next lever (floor still-missing *required slots* directly, analogous to how semantic_plan_margin_decode_weight already floors still-required *plan families*) will raise required-slot coverage and/or downstream quality metrics beyond the existing generic schema_role_slot_decode_weight bias, at a reasonable margin scale, without harming structure when the margin is too strong.

wiring: --required-slot-margin-decode-weight CLI flag added new to scripts/train_model.py (this family of decode-weight flags did not exist at all on train_model.py's CLI at this branch tip; PR #623's claimed backfill has not landed here, so only this one flag was added, matching evaluate_model.py's style)
added to the E617 contract-gated ValueError guard list (self._slot_contracts must be populated via --slot-contract-constrained-decode or --template-fill-decode, or the bias silently no-ops)
implementation.semantics: Among the current position's grammar-legal candidates, find slot tokens whose visible slot has not appeared anywhere in the emitted prefix yet (genuinely still missing, unlike schema_role_slot_decode_weight's flat bonus for any role-compatible slot whether filled or not). Floor the best-scoring still-missing slot candidate to (max legal candidate score + margin), mirroring semantic_plan_margin_decode_weight's flooring of still-required plan families over the component-candidate ceiling. No-ops when the weight is 0, when there is no slot contract, or once every visible slot in the legal candidate set has already been emitted.
implementation.unit_tests: tests/test_models/test_compiler_decode.py::test_required_slot_margin_bias_floors_only_still_missing_slots (fires only for a genuinely-missing slot, no-ops once fully covered, no-ops at weight 0, no-ops with no slot contract) + added to the parametrized test_contract_gated_decode_weight_without_slot_contract_decode_raises guard

training.note: Reused E620's exact train recipe (same corpus/seed/steps/architecture); last_loss matches E620's 4.0680 to 4 decimal places, confirming an equivalent scratch checkpoint was reproduced independently on this branch.

eval_recipe.note: control_run_id (E626 control, required_slot_margin=0) reproduces E620's treatment (schema_role_slot_decode_weight=8) numbers and per-record predictions exactly, confirming the full E617-era recipe (schema_role_slot_decode_weight and the semantic_plan_* family) was correctly replayed before adding the new lever.

decision: Real, honest, dose-dependent result on a real n=4 OOD replay against a freshly-trained scratch checkpoint that reproduces E620's exact recipe and treatment numbers. A moderate margin (2.0, matching the scale of the other margin_decode_weight levers already active in this recipe) raises every headline quality metric except AST edge F1 (fidelity +0.28, validity +0.17, structure +0.06, recall +0.08, reward +0.09, AST node F1 +0.07, meaningful v1 +0.25), and Dashboard/Auth both gain additional correctly-typed components/slots they previously omitted. A strong margin (6.0) instead regresses every headline metric well below control (fidelity -0.25, validity -0.25, reward -0.24), collapsing Dashboard to a single bare Button and leaving Gallery's typed array empty again -- evidence that an overly large floor can hijack early structural (component-choice) decisions away from correct component selection, not just fill slots. binding_aware_meaningful_v2_rate_strict (strict v2) stays 0.0 in all three arms: required_placeholder_missing and/or placeholder_semantic_role_mismatch still fire on at least one record in every arm, so this checkpoint does not yet clear strict meaning even at the best-performing margin. Retain required_slot_margin_decode_weight as a promising default-off lever at a moderate margin scale (~2.0, matching sibling margin weights); do NOT adopt margin=6 as a default. This is a single small (n=4) matched-pair replay on one scratch checkpoint, not a confirmatory suite -- not a ship claim. No checkpoint promoted or synced.
next_step: Run a powered/confirmatory multi-seed replay (see H19's protocol, docs/design/quality-experiment-matrix.md) sweeping required_slot_margin_decode_weight across a small grid (e.g. 1, 2, 3, 4) on a full held-out/rico_held suite, not just n=4 OOD, before considering this margin for a default recipe change; also trace *why* margin=6 hijacks the Dashboard root-component choice (is the bias only supposed to fire on slot-token candidates, or is it interacting with another lever's flooring at the same position?) since a decode-time bias regressing structure by over-flooring a slot candidate above a correct component candidate is itself worth a root-cause iteration, analogous to E616/E617's upstream-gap chase.
